seo.title: Frikkadelle with cabbage - comfort food - Marinda Kook

html:
I know cabbage frikkadelle, but not everyone in our house eats cabbage. So I made the two separately. There is something about frikkadelle that comforts. Something that invites you in with a warmth of food to enjoy.

This recipe is enough for 8 people. Halve everything if there are fewer of you. I made extra for tomorrow too.

  • 1kg beef mince
  • 500g pork mince – I used pork bangers and squeezed them out
  • 1 slice of bread soaked in 30ml milk
  • 1 onion, grated
  • 2 eggs, whisked with a fork
  • 10 ml salt
  • 5 ml pepper
  • 5 ml ground nutmeg
  • 60 ml Worcestershire sauce
  • 60 ml chutney
  • 5 ml coriander (leave out if you don't have it)
  • 80 ml cake flour
  • a little oil

NOW:

  1. in a mixing bowl combine the beef mince and the pork – squeeze the pork out of the casing
  2. soak the bread in the milk. Squeeze out all the milk and crumble the bread onto the meat.
  3. whisk the eggs and add to the meat
  4. grate the onion and add to your meat
  5. now add all the rest of the ingredients except the oil
  6. mix it well with your hands – nothing works better than your hands
  7. pour a little oil into the bottom of your oven dish
  8. shape frikkadelle with your hands and pack them onto the oil
  9. bake for around 1 hour uncovered at 190 degrees Celsius

CABBAGE:

Fry one onion in a little oil on the stove top in a pot. Slice your cabbage into strips and add it to the fried onion. Add one tin of tomato and onion mix. (I used the one with chilli in it) Now add salt, pepper, nutmeg, Worcestershire sauce and the juice of one lemon – do this according to taste. Fry everything together until soft but not mushy.

Now enjoy your frikkadelle and cabbage and make sure everyone in the house eats …….
